nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; generally observed in small groups along reef edges and rubble patches, with juveniles tending toward shallower depths

diet.notes: Generally accepts vitamin-enriched frozen mysis shrimp and brine shrimp, along with high-quality marine flake and pellet foods. Feeding 2–3 small portions per day has been recommended to support its active metabolism.

summary: A vividly colored, peaceful fairy wrasse from Indonesia with a distinctive orange-to-yellow dorsal marking; generally considered reef-safe and well suited to mature community reef aquariums.
careNotes: Cirrhilabrus aurantidorsalis is endemic to Indonesia in the Western Central Pacific, where it has been documented occurring in small groups along reef edges and rubble patches at depths of roughly 10–25 m. Adult size figures show a minor cross-source discrepancy: FishBase records a maximum of 10.0 cm TL while LiveAquaria lists 4.5 in (~11.4 cm); the range 9–11.5 cm brackets both figures. Like other fairy wrasses, this species is a well-documented jumper and a tight-fitting lid or eurobrace is strongly advised. Colors are reported to intensify markedly during courtship displays, with males showing a more vivid orange back and a dark-red crown-like marking on the head. Best kept as a single male or as one male with one or more females added simultaneously; introducing females after the male is established may provoke aggression. As a protogynous hermaphrodite, individuals start as females and the dominant individual transitions to male. Conspecific male-to-male aggression is typical of the genus; only one male per tank is generally recommended unless the aquarium is large enough to establish separate territories. Will not harm corals or ornamental invertebrates and is commonly considered an excellent reef-safe addition.
breedingNotes: Captive spawning is rarely documented. FishBase notes distinct pairing during breeding in the wild, consistent with the harem-spawning social structure typical of Cirrhilabrus. Providing a harem group (one male, two or more females) in a sufficiently large aquarium may encourage nuptial display behavior.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species product page used for care level (Easy), 90-gal tank recommendation, temperature (72–78°F), salinity (1.020–1.025), pH (8.1–8.4), carnivore diet, peaceful temperament, reef-safe status, adult size (4.5 in), and jumper warning.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked: confirmed max TL 10.0 cm (vs. LiveAquaria 11.4 cm — minor discrepancy, averaged to 9–11.5 cm range), family Labridae, order Eupercaria, endemic to Indonesia (Western Central Pacific), depth 10–25 m, reef-associated habitat with rubble patches, distinct pairing during breeding, IUCN Least Concern.
